# Finalize v13 release-note narrative arc

## Context

v13 release-note work is intentionally iterative under #12696. The current release artifact now has strong narrative chapters, appendix/SEO/upgrade-path work in flight, and #12733 contains an insert-ready curated changelog digest draft. The operator's active direction remains: once the content is there, narrative arc and storytelling matter, and the final artifact must match the bar set by `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v12.1.0.md` and `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v11.24.0.md`.

This ticket exists to capture the final editorial/narrative pass as a concrete release sub, not as an ambient chat reminder.

V-B-A evidence collected on 2026-06-08:

- Live latest-open sweep: checked latest 20 open issues via GitHub API at creation time; no equivalent final v13 narrative-arc / editorial-polish ticket found. Nearby items are #12733 (curated changelog digest), #12729 (upgrade path), #12727 (SEO routes), #12725 (identity scale proof), and #12723 (appendix evidence).
- Exact local sweep across `resources/content/issues`, `resources/content/archive/issues`, and `resources/content/discussions` for `final narrative`, `narrative arc`, `storytelling pass`, `release-note narrative`, and `v13.*narrative` found Discussion #12694 plus older v12 precedent tickets (#9577, #9345), but no active v13 final-pass ticket.
- KB synthesis sweep via `ask_knowledge_base(type='ticket')` was attempted and failed with Gemini 429 quota; fallback non-generative KB search for `v13 release notes final narrative arc storytelling pass` surfaced older archived release-note tickets and no current v13 duplicate.
- Source comparison: current `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` still has `## Full Changelog Strategy` and `## Release-Cut Checklist`; the v12.1/v11.24 benchmark notes both have concrete categorized `## Full Changelog` sections and a tighter final narrative shape.
- #12733 now carries an insert-ready digest draft, but the file patch is gated on #12724/#12730 merging to avoid same-file PR stacking.

## The Problem

The v13 release note is accumulating the right content, but the final release artifact still needs a dedicated narrative-arc pass after the content subs land.

Without that pass, v13 risks two opposite failures:

1. Remaining a set of strong but separately-authored chapters, not one major-release story.
2. Over-correcting into polish before the content is complete, which would bury the actual v13 proof: 1,200+ merged PRs / 1,600+ closed issues, the Native Edge Graph, Dream Pipeline, Memory Core, swarm governance, AiConfig, cloud/multi-tenant work, and the still-honest Body/Grid boundary.

The final pass should happen late, after the content layers are present: appendix evidence, SEO/route ingestion, upgrade path, curated digest, identity proof, and the latest grid boundary.

## The Architectural Reality

Release notes are public documentation and ingestion substrate. They are consumed by humans, portal/SEO routes, Knowledge Base ingestion, future release-workflow agents, and the Native Edge Graph.

Relevant current surfaces:

- `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` — current v13 release artifact.
- `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v12.1.0.md` — benchmark major/minor release note with phased narrative plus categorized `Full Changelog`.
- `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v11.24.0.md` — benchmark concise release note with strong case-study sections plus categorized `Full Changelog`.
- #12696 — parent v13 release epic.
- #12694 — source discussion for curated v13 narrative before release-skill codification.
- #12733 — curated changelog digest section.
- #12723, #12727, #12729, #12725 — appendix/SEO/upgrade/identity feed tickets and PRs.
- #9491, #9872, #9492 — Body/Grid boundary tickets that must not be over-claimed while open.

## The Fix

After the content feed PRs land, perform one final narrative-arc pass over `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md`.

The pass should:

- Ensure the opening thesis, chapter order, diagrams, war stories, curated digest, generated appendix strategy, and release-cut checklist read as one major-release artifact.
- Compare directly against `v12.1.0.md` and `v11.24.0.md`, using them as bar-setting references rather than templates to copy mechanically.
- Tighten repeated anchor lists so the digest and narrative chapters complement each other instead of duplicating the same PR bullets.
- Keep v13's main identity as self-evolving software organism / Brain + Body + Swarm + MX loop, not a narrow "Agent OS maturity" headline.
- Refresh live counts and all gate-dependent wording immediately before the PR.
- Keep Body/Grid wording honest until #9491, #9872, and #9492 are terminal.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` narrative body | #12696, #12694, current release note, v12.1/v11.24 benchmarks | Final artifact reads as one coherent major-release story with content-complete chapters, digest, appendix strategy, and release checklist | If content feed PRs are still open, defer the final pass and leave a visible gate comment | Same file | PR diff plus benchmark heading/content comparison |
| Curated digest / appendix boundary | #12733 and Discussion #12694 | Digest provides scan-friendly representative bullets; exhaustive corpus remains generated/reproducible | If counts are stale, make digest qualitative and keep final count refresh in checklist | Same file | Digest section present and appendix command retained |
| Identity / scale proof | ADR 0018 / identity SSOT, #12725 / PR #12726 once resolved | Use only confirmed canonical proof-point basis and terminal merged evidence | If #12726 remains unresolved, avoid treating it as shipped final evidence | Same file | Live PR/issue state and source-of-truth check |
| Body/Grid section | #9491, #9872, #9492, #12708 | Mention landed SortZone evidence without claiming final View-owned multi-body grid architecture until remaining tickets land | Keep explicit guardrail / release-cut checklist item | Same file | Live issue/PR state before final PR |
| Portal / SEO / ingestion consumers | #12727 and release-note build/index scripts | Final note renders and remains indexed through existing release-note pipeline | If route PRs remain open, delay final polish PR or stack only with explicit operator approval | PR body | Release-note build/index verification |

## Decision Record impact

none. This is a release artifact editorial/content-completion pass. It should honor ADR 0018 and ADR 0019 where cited, but it does not amend either decision record.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] v13 release notes include a concrete curated digest or equivalent scan layer before final narrative polish is treated as complete.
- [ ] Final pass compares the resulting artifact against `v12.1.0.md` and `v11.24.0.md` and records the comparison in the PR evidence.
- [ ] Opening thesis, chapter order, diagrams, digest, appendix strategy, and release-cut checklist read as one coherent major-release artifact.
- [ ] Repeated anchor lists are deduped or intentionally separated between narrative chapters and digest categories.
- [ ] Live merged-PR / closed-issue counts are refreshed immediately before the PR or explicitly marked as release-cut checklist items if the release line remains open.
- [ ] Identity proof wording follows the terminal source-of-truth state for #12725/#12726.
- [ ] Body/Grid wording is rechecked against #9491, #9872, and #9492 immediately before PR open.
- [ ] Release-note build/index verification is run after the edit.

## Out of Scope

- Adding the curated changelog digest itself if #12733 is still open; this ticket is the final pass after that content lands.
- Authoring the future release-notes skill; #10321 remains parked until the v13 artifact exposes the repeatable workflow.
- Human-only release publication / `main` release-line mutation.
- Reopening the peer-selection-model-per-body grid design.
- A raw 1,200+ PR mega-changelog in the narrative body.

## Avoided Traps / Gold Standards Rejected

- Reject premature polish: narrative arc last means after the content feed exists, not before.
- Reject template-copying v12.1/v11.24: they set the bar, but v13 is an anomalous bridge release and needs its own shape.
- Reject over-claiming grid completion: #12708 is landed evidence; #9491, #9872, and #9492 are still gate checks until terminal.
- Reject treating identity proof counts as free prose while #12726 is still pending source-of-truth confirmation.
